prompt: The accountants doing monthly close keep asking me "what was the checking balance on the 31st?" and right now ledger.py can only answer with today's balance, so I've been eyeballing history() output like a caveman. Please add point-in-time queries: balance_asof(account, on) giving the balance counting every entry dated on-or-before `on`, and balances_asof(on) doing that for every open account in one dict (accounts with nothing posted yet should show 0, not be missing — their spreadsheet import breaks on missing keys). Watch out: we post backdated corrections all the time, so entries are NOT in date order internally — an entry posted last week but dated in January must count toward a January as-of balance. Same date as the cutoff counts as included, that's how statements work. Validation should match post(): unknown account is a KeyError, and a date that isn't YYYY-MM-DD (or isn't a string at all) is a ValueError for both new methods. Nothing about post/balance/balances/history may change behavior. Contract is test_ledger.py — python3 test_ledger.py must pass, and don't edit that file.
